title: cyperus malaccensis subsp. malaccensis
intro: Cyperus malaccensis subsp. malaccensis, also known as the Malayan umbrella sedge, is a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Cyperus malaccensis subsp. malaccensis include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating wounds, as well as its potential in managing diabetes and hypertension. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine.
